name: Dell S2722QC 27-Inch 4K USB-C Monitor
whatWorks: At around $449 CAD, the S2722QC brings 4K IPS quality and USB-C with 65W Power Delivery to a price point that previously required accepting 1440p or compromising on connectivity. For most office setups, this is the right amount of monitor at the right price.

The USB-C port with 65W Power Delivery handles both display and laptop charging through a single cable — clean desk setups with a single cable connecting laptop to monitor work perfectly on MacBook Air, Dell XPS 13, and most USB-C ultrabooks.

The 99% sRGB coverage and IPS panel produce accurate, consistent colors across the screen with excellent viewing angles. Document work, photo review, and video calls all look sharp and true, making this a genuine step up from budget FHD monitors.

The stand adjustment covers height, tilt, and swivel but not pivot to portrait. For users who work in landscape orientation exclusively, the ergonomic range is sufficient. The stand is study and doesn't creep once set.
worthKnowing: The stand does not pivot to portrait orientation, which matters for developers and readers who value portrait mode for long documents. The VESA mount (100×100) accepts aftermarket arms if portrait is important.

HDR400 certification means the peak brightness for HDR content is 400 nits, which produces a modest HDR effect — better than no HDR but not comparable to true HDR monitors with local dimming above 600 nits.
The 60Hz refresh rate is standard for a productivity monitor at this price. If you want 4K and high refresh rate, you'll need to spend significantly more for 4K@144Hz panels.

Some early units had a uniformity issue with slight brightness variation in corners. This is less consistent than Dell's higher-end U-series monitors; if you receive a unit with noticeable uniformity problems, Dell's support for replacements is generally responsive.

commonComplaints.title: No portrait pivot…
commonComplaints.body: Great monitor but no portrait mode is a real limitation. I rotate monitors frequently for reading code and documents — this requires an aftermarket arm, which adds cost.

commonComplaints.title: Only 60Hz…
commonComplaints.body: Expected for a 4K productivity monitor at this price, but still disappointing. Once you've used 120Hz you notice 60Hz immediately.

commonComplaints.title: HDR is mediocre…
commonComplaints.body: HDR400 looks barely different from SDR. Not a major issue for office work but don't buy this expecting impressive HDR for movie watching.

commonComplaints.title: Stand feels slightly wobbly…
commonComplaints.body: The stand has a little more flex than I'd like at full height extension. Stable once set, but adjusting height causes more wobble than the U-series Dell stands.

verdict: The Dell S2722QC is the most accessible 4K USB-C monitor for Canadian buyers who want single-cable laptop connectivity at a reasonable price. The IPS panel with 99% sRGB is genuinely accurate, the USB-C at 65W handles most ultrabooks, and 4K at 27 inches delivers meaningfully sharper text than 1440p. The lack of portrait pivot and 60Hz ceiling are the main limitations, but for pure office productivity this is an excellent choice at its price point.

realBuyers.quote: 4K sharpness is a revelation for text and spreadsheets. I can fit so much more on screen than my old 1080p 27-inch and the text is noticeably crisper.
Single USB-C cable from my MacBook Air runs the 4K display and charges at 65W simultaneously. Exactly the clean desk setup I wanted.
Colors look great, especially for photos and creative work. For $449 the IPS panel is well above what I expected at this price.
Moved from a 1080p 24-inch. The combination of larger size and 4K resolution makes reading documents and spreadsheets significantly easier on my eyes.

metaDescription: Dell S2722QC 27-inch 4K monitor review for Canada: USB-C 65W PD, 99% sRGB, IPS. Best affordable 4K USB-C monitor. ~$449 CAD on Amazon.ca.
introText: The Dell S2722QC (ASIN B094BJNWNV) is a 27-inch 4K IPS monitor with USB-C 65W Power Delivery and 99% sRGB coverage, available on Amazon.ca at around $449 CAD.
